title: Buying EURC (EURC): Safest Exchanges and Steps
content:
EURC is an essential tool for those looking to maintain exposure to the euro while navigating the blockchain ecosystem. By providing a transparent, regulated, and fiat-backed stablecoin, Circle has created a bridge similar to the role [USDC](/usd-coin) plays for the dollar. | Metric | Details | |

---

|

---

| | Current Price | $1.17 | | Market Cap | $435.28M | | Market Rank | #111 | | 24H Change | +0.02% | | Date | April 29, 2026 |

## Why Buy EURC? EURC allows users to hedge against USD volatility or settle trades directly in euros on-chain. It is particularly useful for European traders looking to minimize conversion friction when interacting with DeFi protocols or platforms that support euro-denominated pairs.

## Top Exchange Options Most major centralized exchanges support EURC due to its regulatory compliance. You can typically find it paired against other major assets. When choosing an exchange, look for high liquidity to minimize slippage, as liquidity can vary compared to more liquid assets like Chainlink.

## Purchase Tutorial 1. Sign up for a reputable exchange that supports EURC. 2. Complete the necessary KYC (Know Your Customer) identity verification. 3. Deposit fiat currency (EUR) into your exchange account. 4. Navigate to the trading section and locate the EURC/EUR or EURC/USDC trading pair. 5. Execute your order according to your personal financial strategy. 6. Withdraw your tokens to a private wallet for long-term storage.

## Securing Your Tokens Storing assets on an exchange carries inherent risks. For long-term holdings, use a non-custodial hardware wallet. These devices keep your private keys offline, protecting you from exchange-level vulnerabilities. Always double-check the contract address before sending funds.

## Important Risks As noted in our TokenRadar analysis, EURC has a Risk Score of 5. While it is stable, all on-chain assets carry technical risks, including potential smart contract vulnerabilities. Ensure you are aware of the network fees associated with your chosen chain, such as Ethereum or Avalanche, before initiating transactions.
